description: Default SSL verification is set to True, but the library allows disabling it via verify=False. This is a known security anti-pattern that makes applications vulnerable to MITM attacks. The library should enforce certificate verification by default and provide clearer warnings about the risks of disabling it.
suggested_fix: Add a prominent warning in the docstring and raise a UserWarning when verify=False is used. Consider deprecating the ability to disable verification entirely in future versions.

description: The max_redirects check raises TooManyRedirects but doesn't clean up resources properly. The response object may still hold connections open, leading to resource leaks.
suggested_fix: Ensure resp.close() is called before raising the exception, and consider using a try/finally block to guarantee cleanup.

description: get_netrc_auth() is called with trust_env=True, which may expose credentials stored in .netrc files. These files often have weak permissions and can be read by other users on the system.
suggested_fix: Add a warning about .netrc security implications, or provide an option to disable .netrc auth entirely via an environment variable or parameter.

description: resolve_proxies() with trust_env=True may expose proxy credentials stored in environment variables. These can be leaked through logs, process tables, or to other applications sharing the same environment.
suggested_fix: Add a configuration option to clear proxy environment variables after use, or provide a secure way to handle proxy credentials (e.g., encrypted storage).

description: The _encode_files method has complex nested conditionals and manual file handling that could be simplified. The logic for handling different file tuple formats (2-tuple, 3-tuple, 4-tuple) is error-prone and hard to maintain.
suggested_fix: Refactor into smaller, well-named helper functions. Add comprehensive type checking and clearer error messages for invalid file formats.

description: The prepare_body method calls super_len() multiple times on streamable bodies, which can be expensive for large files. The length calculation could be cached or avoided when not needed.
suggested_fix: Cache the result of super_len() in a local variable, or only calculate it when necessary (e.g., when setting Content-Length).

description: The super_len() function has complex branching logic to handle various file-like objects. It tries multiple approaches (len(), .len, fileno, tell) which makes it hard to understand and maintain.
suggested_fix: Break into smaller helper functions for each case (e.g., _len_from_len(), _len_from_fileno(), etc.). Add more comprehensive docstrings explaining each branch.

description: The get_netrc_auth() function catches NetrcParseError and OSError but continues silently. If netrc parsing fails, the user is never notified, which could lead to authentication failures without explanation.
suggested_fix: Log a warning or raise a custom exception when netrc parsing fails, unless raise_errors is explicitly set to False.

description: The should_bypass_proxies() function calls getproxies_environment() and proxy_bypass_registry() which may be expensive operations (especially reading from the Windows registry). These are called on every request.
suggested_fix: Cache the proxy environment results, or allow them to be set once at session initialization rather than on every request.

description: The resolve_proxies() function copies the proxies dictionary and then modifies it. This creates unnecessary memory allocations on every request.
suggested_fix: Instead of copying the entire dictionary, create a new dictionary with only the modified entries, or use immutable mapping patterns.

description: The default_headers() function includes a User-Agent that reveals the Python version and requests library version. This information could be used by attackers to fingerprint the application and target known vulnerabilities.
suggested_fix: Allow customization of the User-Agent, or provide an option to use a generic or randomized User-Agent by default.

description: The __init__ method of Session has inline comments that are redundant and make the code harder to read. For example, '# Bootstrap CookieJar.' and '# Merge with session cookies' could be moved to docstrings or separate functions.
suggested_fix: Refactor the initialization logic into well-named private methods with proper docstrings, removing the inline comments.

description: The prepare_url method raises MissingSchema with a suggestion to use https://, but this could be problematic for non-HTTP URLs (e.g., ftp, file). The check is too focused on HTTP/HTTPS.
suggested_fix: Make the schema validation more generic to support other protocols, or provide a way to opt-out of this behavior for custom schemes.

description: The get_encoding_from_headers() function has a hardcoded fallback to ISO-8859-1 for any 'text/*' content type. This is outdated and may cause mojibake for modern applications that use UTF-8 by default.
suggested_fix: Update the fallback to prefer UTF-8, or make the encoding detection more sophisticated by considering the content or other headers.

description: The rebuild_proxies method is called on every redirect and extracts username/password from the proxy URL using basic parsing. This could be optimized by caching the parsed result.
suggested_fix: Cache the parsed proxy credentials in a dictionary keyed by the proxy URL, with proper invalidation when proxies are updated.
